summary: FastGPTMini setup.py drops and executes remote.bat from anonymous file host at install
details: On `pip install FastGPTMini`, setup.py fetches https://tmpfiles.org/dl/wNwdULF0K8NS/vwchmx.txt, writes the response to `vwchmx.bat` in the current working directory, and runs `cmd /c vwchmx.bat`, executing attacker-controlled Windows shell code on the installer's machine. The fetch is unpinned, unverified, and served from tmpfiles.org — an anonymous throwaway file host with no publisher accountability. After the dropper runs, setup.py prints a fabricated 'admin privileges required' error to disguise the install as a benign failure. The package itself is a hollow lure: PKG-INFO shows placeholder metadata (Name/Author/Summary all set to `FastGPTMini`), and the `src/` tree contains only egg-info with no Python modules — the entire artifact exists to deliver the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
